response_summary.recommended_bid_note: recommended_bid is a phase-aware model clearing target (star floors, pitcher dampening, isotonic smoothing)—a bidding guide, not a prediction of the winning hammer price; room behavior can diverge materially.
response_summary.team_adjusted_value_note: team_adjusted_value scales adjusted_value by roster need, dollars per open slot vs league peers, remaining-slot scarcity, and replacement drop-off for eligible slots; when the league snapshot is symmetric (no auction picks, no keeper/minors/taxi off-board ids, equal per-team budgets in budget_by_team_id when provided, equal rostered counts per team), it equals adjusted_value
market_notes: Market is near auction-open baseline and 1B is the key position to monitor.
1B: No Tier 1 1B players remaining — act immediately.
2B: No Tier 1 2B players remaining — act immediately.
3B: No Tier 1 3B players remaining — act immediately.

position_alerts.message: OF supply is stable at the moment.
position_alerts.recommended_action: No urgency at OF; stay value-driven.

assumptions: Auction inflation used replacement_slots_v2: greedy league-wide slot fill yields per-slot replacement baselines; surplus cash maps to value above replacement (see docs/valuation-inflation-semantics.md). inflation_percent_vs_auction_open and inflation_index_vs_opening_auction (1.00×) describe change vs a replayed auction-open state; inflation_percent_vs_neutral is always (inflation_factor−1)×100 (allocator vs 1.0, not "list neutral").
The inflation factor may be clamped to a workflow floor/cap when list dollars versus remaining cash are extreme.
This response used inflation_raw=38.8426 before clamp; applied factor is 3.0000 (cap).
Scarcity urgency uses remaining elite and mid-tier supply versus league demand.
Draft state is treated as stateless full-context input for every request.
players_remaining is always the count of the full undrafted pool; valuations.length may be smaller when player_ids filters the response.
pool_value_remaining equals total_surplus_mass (sum of marginal surplus $ in the slot-assigned draftable pool).
Real auction accuracy depends on catalog value quality and id/name alignment with the player pool.

recommended_bid is a clearing-style guide (phase- and list-informed); it is not framed as the exact price the room will pay—compare to adjusted_value and team_adjusted_value for surplus vs roster context.

sample_valuations.name: Player_1
why: ADP and model auction value are broadly aligned.
Adjusted auction $8.2 vs baseline $108.0 after league inflation (3.00×).
Roster/scarcity is reflected in baseline list price (scarcity_component=0.200 on baseline_components), before the league inflation factor.
Elite tier in an inflated market — expect competitive bidding.

drivers.label: League inflation
drivers.reason: replacement_slots_v2: slot-aware surplus allocation (factor 3.00× on marginal list $ above replacement); see response replacement_values_by_slot_or_position and fallback_reason. Auction-open index 1.00× (1.0 = same allocator at replayed auction open). Raw ratio was 38.84× before the workflow cap.

drivers.label: Scarcity context (OF)
drivers.reason: Scarcity multiplier 0.200 is embedded in list_value; scarcity_adjustment stays 0 so drivers reconcile to auction_target − list_value.

drivers.label: Other model effects
drivers.reason: No residual; Steal/Reach comes from ADP vs value rank, not an extra dollar line item.
